hero.headline: Assam Don Bosco University Online MBA in Marketing Management: Fees, Syllabus and Career Scope
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved 2-year programme | NAAC A accredited | Don Bosco/Salesian educational network

sections.tldr: Assam Don Bosco University Online MBA in Marketing Management covers Sales and Distribution Management, Marketing Communication, Service Marketing, Internet Marketing, and International Marketing in Sem 3. Sem 4 includes Consumer Behaviour, Retail Management, Tourism and Hospitality Management, Product and Brand Management, and Customer Relationship Management. NAAC A accredited, UGC-DEB approved. Part of the Don Bosco/Salesian educational network. Startup Management is a common Sem 3 core.

about.body:
Assam Don Bosco University (CDOE) is a private university in Guwahati, Assam, operating under the globally recognized Don Bosco/Salesian educational network. The Salesian order runs more than 1,500 educational institutions across 130+ countries. ADB holds NAAC A accreditation and UGC-DEB approval for its online programmes.

The Marketing Management specialisation places five dedicated subjects in each semester. Sem 3 covers Sales and Distribution Management, Marketing Communication, Service Marketing, Internet Marketing, and International Marketing. Sem 4 includes Consumer Behaviour, Retail Management, Tourism and Hospitality Management, Product and Brand Management, and Customer Relationship Management.

Tourism and Hospitality Management as a Sem 4 subject is notable. NE India is a growing tourism destination (Kaziranga, Meghalaya, Sikkim circuit), and marketing skills for the hospitality sector align with the regional economic context.

Startup Management appears as a common Sem 3 core subject across all ADB MBA specialisations. For marketing professionals, this builds skills in go-to-market strategy, early-stage brand building, and growth marketing for startups.

EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Semesters 1-2 cover the common MBA core. Semesters 3-4 below show the Marketing Management specialisation subjects along with common Sem 3-4 core subjects.

subjects.name: Sales and Distribution Management
subjects.desc: Sales force design, territory management, distribution channel strategy, channel conflict resolution, and sales performance metrics.

subjects.name: Marketing Communication
subjects.desc: IMC planning, advertising strategy, media planning, public relations, sales promotion tools, and digital communication channels.

subjects.name: Service Marketing
subjects.desc: Services marketing mix (7Ps), service quality measurement (SERVQUAL), service blueprinting, customer experience design, and service recovery.

subjects.name: Internet Marketing
subjects.desc: Digital marketing strategy, SEO/SEM basics, social media marketing, email marketing, content marketing, and web analytics.

subjects.name: International Marketing
subjects.desc: Global market entry strategies, export marketing, international pricing, cross-cultural consumer behaviour, and global brand management.

subjects.name: Minor Project
subjects.desc: Guided research project on a marketing-related topic. Problem identification, methodology, and initial findings.

subjects.name: Startup Management
subjects.desc: Startup ecosystem, business model canvas, funding stages (seed, angel, VC), lean startup methodology, and go-to-market strategy.

subjects.name: Operations Management
subjects.desc: Production planning, quality management, supply chain basics, inventory control, and process optimization.

subjects.name: Consumer Behaviour
subjects.desc: Consumer decision-making process, psychological and sociological influences, buying behaviour models, and consumer research methods.

subjects.name: Retail Management
subjects.desc: Retail formats, store operations, visual merchandising, category management, private label strategy, and omnichannel retail.

subjects.name: Tourism and Hospitality Management
subjects.desc: Tourism marketing strategy, destination branding, hospitality service design, tour operator management, and eco-tourism promotion.

subjects.name: Product and Brand Management
subjects.desc: Product lifecycle management, brand architecture, brand equity measurement, new product launch strategy, and brand repositioning.

subjects.name: Customer Relationship Management
subjects.desc: CRM systems and platforms, customer lifecycle management, loyalty programme design, customer retention strategies, and CRM analytics.

subjects.name: Business Policy and Strategic Management
subjects.desc: Strategic analysis frameworks, competitive positioning, corporate-level strategy, and strategic implementation and control.

subjects.name: Research Project/Dissertation
subjects.desc: Full research dissertation on a marketing topic. Data collection, analysis, findings, and structured thesis presentation.

subjects.name: Computer Applications in Business
subjects.desc: Business software tools, data analysis using spreadsheets, MIS reporting, and technology-driven decision support systems.

whoHires.body:
ADB MBA Marketing Management graduates target roles across brand management, digital marketing, sales, and tourism marketing:

**Industries:** FMCG, e-commerce, retail, tourism and hospitality, IT/ITES marketing teams, media and advertising, telecom, and government tourism boards

**Entry-level roles:** Marketing Executive, Brand Associate, Digital Marketing Analyst, Sales Officer, Social Media Coordinator, Tourism Marketing Associate

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years):** Marketing Manager, Brand Manager, Digital Marketing Manager, Area Sales Manager, CRM Manager, Tourism Marketing Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** CMO, VP Marketing, Head of Digital, National Sales Head, Director of Brand Strategy, Head of Tourism Marketing

**Employers:** FMCG companies, e-commerce platforms, retail chains, NE India tourism operators, tea industry marketing teams, and state tourism development corporations.

**Note:** NE India's growing tourism sector (Kaziranga, Meghalaya caves, Sikkim circuit) creates marketing opportunities. Tourism and Hospitality Management as a standalone subject directly aligns with this regional demand.

skills.body:
The Marketing Management specialisation builds skills across brand management, digital marketing, and customer analytics:

**Technical skills:**
- Digital Marketing: SEO/SEM execution, social media campaign management, email marketing automation, content strategy, and Google Analytics interpretation
- Brand Management: brand equity measurement (Keller model), brand architecture design, positioning strategy, and new product launch planning
- CRM: customer segmentation, loyalty programme ROI analysis, CRM platform configuration, and customer lifetime value computation
- Consumer Research: survey design, focus group facilitation, buying behaviour analysis, and market research report writing

**Applied skills:**
- Sales Management: territory planning, sales forecasting, distribution channel design, and sales team performance dashboards
- Tourism Marketing: destination branding, experience package design, eco-tourism promotion strategy, and hospitality service marketing

**Soft skills:** Presenting marketing plans to leadership, negotiating with media buyers and channel partners, managing creative agencies, and pitching brand strategies to clients.

comparisons.body:
Comparing ADB Online MBA Marketing Management against peer programmes:

**vs Uttaranchal Online MBA Marketing (Rs 94,000, NAAC A+):** Uttaranchal offers 8 specialisations with NAAC A+ at a fixed Rs 94,000. ADB brings 10 dedicated marketing subjects across Sem 3-4 and Tourism and Hospitality Management as a standalone subject. Uttaranchal wins on fee transparency and NAAC grade. ADB wins on marketing curriculum breadth and Don Bosco heritage.

**vs Sharda Online MBA Marketing (Rs 1,54,500, NAAC A+):** Sharda is significantly more expensive with NCR positioning. ADB offers more dedicated marketing subjects and Don Bosco network recognition at a likely lower fee point. Sharda wins on NCR corporate access for marketing roles. ADB wins on subject count and Salesian heritage.

**vs Galgotias Online MBA Marketing (Rs 86,400, NAAC A+):** Galgotias is budget-tier with NCR location. ADB offers Tourism and Hospitality Management, International Marketing, and Internet Marketing as dedicated subjects, providing broader marketing coverage. Galgotias wins on published fee. ADB wins on curriculum depth and NE India tourism sector alignment.

**vs Vignan Online MBA Marketing (Rs 90,000, NAAC A+):** Vignan offers a flat Rs 90,000 fee with South India focus. ADB offers broader marketing subject coverage including tourism and hospitality. Both are budget-tier. Choose based on regional preference and curriculum specialisation.

fees.body:
The Assam Don Bosco University Online MBA in Marketing Management fee structure is confirmed at admission. Please reconfirm current fees, payment options, and any applicable charges with our counsellor before enrolment.

Assam Don Bosco University is a UGC-DEB approved NAAC A accredited institution in Guwahati, part of the globally recognized Don Bosco/Salesian educational network. Financing options including zero-cost EMI may be available. Confirm terms with counsellor.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with ADB University each quarter. Fees listed on our pages are indicative pending verification each admission cycle.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive an MBA degree certificate with Marketing Management as the specialisation, issued by Assam Don Bosco University under UGC-DEB online mode standards.

The degree carries NAAC A accreditation and UGC-DEB approval. The university is part of the Don Bosco/Salesian educational network with global recognition across 130+ countries. Valid for government recruitment, PSU roles, corporate marketing positions, and professional marketing certifications eligibility.

reviews.intro: Three reviews from ADB Online MBA Marketing Management students. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Bijoy
items.liked: Don Bosco heritage gives the degree a recognition advantage. Tourism and Hospitality Management is directly relevant for NE India's growing tourism industry
items.disliked: Fee is not publicly listed. Had to contact the admissions team for exact numbers
items.body: I work in the hospitality sector in Assam, and ADB's marketing MBA was the only programme with Tourism and Hospitality Management as a dedicated subject at this tier. The Don Bosco name is respected across NE India and internationally. Internet Marketing and CRM covered digital skills I apply daily. Startup Management as a core subject helped me plan a tourism startup on the side.

items.name: Ritika
items.liked: Ten dedicated marketing subjects across two semesters. Product and Brand Management and Consumer Behaviour were immediately applicable in my FMCG role
items.disliked: LMS interface could be more modern. Some video lectures need higher production quality
items.body: ADB's marketing curriculum is surprisingly thorough for a NE India university. Sales and Distribution, Marketing Communication, Internet Marketing, Service Marketing, and International Marketing in one semester is dense but covers everything. Consumer Behaviour and CRM in Sem 4 added analytical depth. The dissertation was demanding but worth it.

items.name: Nilotpal
items.liked: International Marketing covered export marketing concepts useful for NE India's tea and handicraft export sector
items.disliked: National brand recognition is still building. Employers outside NE India may not immediately recognize ADB
items.body: The marketing curriculum covers the essentials and more. Ten dedicated subjects is a strong count. The content quality is adequate. The main limitation is brand recognition beyond the Northeast. In NE India, Don Bosco is a strong name. Nationally, the brand is still emerging. For the likely fee range, the curriculum depth justifies the choice.

faqs.question: What is Assam Don Bosco University's affiliation?
faqs.answer: Assam Don Bosco University is part of the Don Bosco/Salesian educational network, a globally recognized Catholic educational order operating 1,500+ institutions across 130+ countries. The university is a private institution in Guwahati, Assam.

faqs.question: Is ADB Online MBA UGC-DEB approved?
faqs.answer: Yes. Assam Don Bosco University holds UGC-DEB approval for its online programmes. The MBA degree is legally equivalent to a campus MBA under UGC 2020 guidelines.

faqs.question: Why is the fee structure confirmed at admission?
faqs.answer: ADB's fee structure varies by admission cycle and payment plan. The exact fee, semester breakdown, and any applicable charges are confirmed during the admission process. Contact our counsellor for the current cycle's fee details.

faqs.question: Does the Marketing MBA include tourism and hospitality content?
faqs.answer: Yes. Tourism and Hospitality Management is a standalone Semester 4 subject covering tourism marketing strategy, destination branding, hospitality service design, and eco-tourism promotion. Relevant for NE India's growing tourism sector.

faqs.question: How many dedicated marketing subjects does this programme have?
faqs.answer: Ten dedicated marketing subjects across Sem 3 and Sem 4 (five per semester). Coverage spans sales, digital marketing, service marketing, international marketing, consumer behaviour, retail, tourism, branding, and CRM.

chooseIf: Don Bosco/Salesian educational heritage and global network recognition matter to you.
You want 10 dedicated marketing subjects including Tourism and Hospitality Management and Internet Marketing.
You are based in NE India and want a NAAC A accredited local university with UGC-DEB approval.
NE India's tourism and hospitality sector is your target industry.
consider: Uttaranchal at Rs 94,000 with NAAC A+ offers a clear published fee and 8 specialisations.
Galgotias at Rs 86,400 with NAAC A+ provides NCR brand positioning at a lower published fee.
Vignan at Rs 90,000 with NAAC A+ offers a fixed published fee with South India recognition.
If national brand recognition is your top priority, Amity, NMIMS, or MUJ have stronger pan-India positioning.
